summary: The Goshen Township Board of Zoning Appeals held a public hearing on two variance cases. BZA 2025-008 involved a request to keep 11 chickens on a lot less than one acre (where only 4 are permitted), which was approved with the cap at 11 and containment/enclosure conditions. BZA 2025-009 involved a request for a 10-foot height variance to allow a 20-foot pole sign for a Marathon Gas Station at 1415 State Route 28, which was also approved with a condition to dim the sign during nighttime hours.
decisions: BZA 2025-008: Approved variance to allow 11 chickens on a lot less than one acre with conditions that the enclosure and containment must fall within Goshen zoning resolution guidelines and the number is capped at 11
BZA 2025-009: Approved 10-foot height variance allowing a 20-foot pole sign for the Marathon Gas Station with condition that the sign be dimmed as best possible during nighttime hours
Approval of video minutes from June 16th meeting
action_items: Zoning staff to research possible amendments to the small livestock regulations for lots under one acre for future consideration
Zoning staff to post meeting notices with more advanced notice than 12 hours
Zoning staff to follow up on Bray Concrete application and request letter of intent and full plans for property behind Kroger's
key_amounts: $50,000-$60,000 estimated cost of the proposed Marathon sign
Couple million dollars investment in the Marathon store by the owner
0.49 acres - size of chicken variance property at 6736 Goshen Road
3.24 acres - size of Marathon Gas Station property at 1415 State Route 28
98 square feet - proposed sign area (within 100 sq ft maximum)
602 feet of road frontage on State Route 28 for Marathon property
notable_topics: Variance for keeping 11 chickens on a lot under one acre (BZA 2025-008)
Comparison of chicken regulations with Miami Township (6 allowed vs Goshen's 4)
Height variance for 20-foot Marathon Gas Station pole sign (BZA 2025-009)
Electronic messaging center (EMC) on proposed sign and potential driver distraction concerns
Impact of illuminated sign on nearby residential properties
Potential future State Route 28 road widening
Marathon location to include multiple tenants including Blimpie and restaurants
Goshen Warriors community panel on proposed sign
Upcoming Bray Concrete application for property behind Kroger's
attendees: Mr. Pruitt (absent from roll call responses)
Lori Williams (applicant representative, BZA 2025-009, Marathon Gas Station)
Mark Zickler (property owner at 1428 State Route 28, public commenter)
notes: Mr. Pruitt was called during roll call but did not respond, suggesting absence. The BZA discussed that grandfathered signs in the area (such as UDF) appear to be taller than the 10-foot maximum, estimated at 18-22 feet. The applicant for BZA 2025-008 initially requested 11 chickens but expressed willingness to reduce to 8; the board ultimately approved 11. The Marathon applicant indicated that if the 20-foot sign was not approved, they would instead install two 10-foot signs allowed by code. Staff mentioned a pending application from Bray Concrete for a B2-zoned property behind Kroger's that may come before the BZA soon. Meeting adjourned at 7:25 PM.
title: BZA 7-21-2025
